idea-plugin.name: Python Watchpoint
idea-plugin.description:
Adds Python Data breakpoints (watchpoints) support to PyCharm.

Python Watchpoint lets you break on data changes. When a watched local variable or object attribute changes value, the debugger pauses at the mutation line and highlights the exact location

Main entry points:
  • Debug with Watchpoint – starts a debug session with the watchpoint runtime injected.
  • Add Watchpoint – right-click any variable in the Variables panel while paused. The same action toggles to Remove Watchpoint for already watched values.
Highlights:
  • Watch local variables and object attributes for value changes.
  • On hit: pause at the mutation line, with the old → new value shown inline.
  • Watched values are marked in the Variables panel and tracked across stack frames.
  • Low overhead until the first watch is armed (built on Python sys.monitoring).
Current limitations
  • Requires PyCharm 2023.3 or newer.
  • Requires Python 3.12+.
  • Works with PyCharm's pydevd debugger integration only.
  • debugpy is not supported yet.
  • In-place mutation of C-extension objects (NumPy, Pandas, etc.) is not detected – e.g. arr[0] = 99 changes the buffer in C without rebinding the variable or triggering attribute assignment, so there is nothing to observe. Reassigning the whole variable is still caught.
idea-plugin.change-notes: • Main release: initial public release of Python Watchpoint for PyCharm.

action.description: Start debugging with watchpoint support enabled
Pause the debugger whenever the selected variable's value changes
